auth_notes: Requires creating a Dropbox Developer App, configuring redirect URIs for n8n, and selecting specific Paper scopes (e.g., members.read, paper.content.write).
rate_limits: Dropbox uses a tiered rate limiting system; typically allows for thousands of requests per month on free tiers, with specific 'too_many_requests' errors if burst limits are exceeded.
gotchas: Dropbox Paper is transitioning from a standalone format to 'Paper in Dropbox' (standard file system), which may require using the Files API instead of the Paper API for newer documents.
The legacy Paper API uses different ID formats than the standard Dropbox Files API.
Exporting content often requires handling binary streams for PDF or Markdown formats.
research_notes: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node. Use 'OAuth2' for Authentication. Note that the API endpoint base is 'https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/paper/'. For newer accounts, Paper docs are stored as .paper files, so some operations might shift to the standard Dropbox 'files' endpoints.
